<notebookFormatInstructions>
When generating notebook content, use a JSON format. 
1. Each cell must be a valid JSON object within the cells array property with a `metadata.language` property indicating the type of content (e.g., `markdown`, `python`). 
2. Existing cells must contain the `metadata.id` property to uniquely identify each cell. 
3. New cells do not need a `metadata.id` property. 
4. Ensure the content is valid JSON and logically structured. 
5. Do not reference Cell Ids (as users cannot see these values) in user messages, instead use the Cell number (starting from 1). 

Here is sample content of a Notebook document:

<example>
````json
// filepath: /Users/someone/proj01/example.ipynb
{
  cells: [
    {
      cell_type: "markdown",
      metadata: {
          id: "f8939937",
          language: "markdown"
      },
      source: [
          "# Import Required Libraries",
          "Import the necessary libraries, including pandas and plotly."
      ]
    },
    {
      cell_type: "code",
      metadata: {
          id: "0b4e03d1",
          language: "python"
      },
      source: [
          "# Import Required Libraries",
          "import pandas as pd",
          "import plotly.express as px"
      ]
    }
  ]
}
````
</example>

</notebookFormatInstructions>

<attachment id="postInstall" filePath="/root/capsule/environment/postInstall">
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu

echo "==== Installing Visual Studio Code (.deb) ===="

TMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"' EXIT
cd "$TMP_DIR"

fetch() {
  URL="$1"
  OUT="$2"
  if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then  
    curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
  elif command -v wget >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    wget -q "$URL" -O "$OUT"
  else
    if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "curl/wget not found; installing curl..."
      apt-get update
      apt-get install -y curl
      curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
    else
      echo "Error: neither curl nor wget is available, and no apt-get to install them."
      exit 127
    fi
  fi
}

echo "Downloading VS Code .deb package..."
fetch "https://code.visualstudio.com/sha/download?build=stable&os=linux-deb-x64" "vscode.deb"

if ! command -v dpkg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Error: dpkg not found. This script supports Debian/Ubuntu images only."
  exit 1
fi

echo "Installing VS Code..."
# Try install; if deps missing, fix them and retry
if ! dpkg -i vscode.deb 2>/dev/null; then
  if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    apt-get update
    apt-get install -y -f
    dpkg -i vscode.deb
  else
    echo "Error: apt-get not available to resolve dependencies."
    exit 1
  fi
fi

# Simple, build-safe verification (no GUI/sandbox)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1 && dpkg -s code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "VS Code installed successfully:"
  dpkg -s code | awk -F': ' '/^Version:/{print "Version:", $2}'
else
  echo "Error: VS Code installation failed."
  exit 1
fi

# Ensure the folder exists
mkdir -p /root/capsule/code/vscode-data

# Upgrade pip and install Jupyter
echo "Installing/Upgrading pip and Jupyter..."
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip
python3 -m pip install jupyter

# Install VS Code extensions for Python + Jupyter
# (These will go into /root/.vscode-server/extensions or ~/.vscode/extensions)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Installing VS Code Python and Jupyter extensions..."
  code --install-extension ms-python.python --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox || true
  code --install-extension ms-toolsai.jupyter --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox || true
else
  echo "Warning: 'code' command not found — skipping VS Code extension installation."
fi

# Create a system-wide alias for all future shells
# now you can simply run 'code' in Terminal
echo "alias code='code --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox'" >> /etc/bash.bashrc

# installing utilities and cleaning apt caches
apt-get install -y numlockx gthumb || true
rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
apt-get clean || true
rm -rf /var/cache/apt/archives/*

# ensure pip tooling is up-to-date
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel

python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
  pandas \
  seaborn \
  matplotlib \
  scipy \
  tifffile \
  zarr \
  "numpy==1.26.4" \
  "opencv-python-headless==4.11.0.86" \
  codeocean==0.14.0 \
  aind_session \
  pytest \
  pillow \
  pandas \
  pyyaml \
  aind-ophys-utils \
  pymongo \
  ipykernel \
  scikit-learn \
  suite2p \
  pyqtgraph \
  pyqt5 \
  'napari[all]' \
  'pydantic<2.11' \
  plotly \
  anywidget \
  ipympl

cd /
git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/comb || true
cd comb || truecode
pip install -e . || true
#git checkout 21b9cae9431f7f83e1973af6f1fc1ecd40fc765d
git checkout for_gcamp_validation || true

cd /
git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/lamf-analysis || true
cd lamf-analysis || true
pip install -e . || true

# Update Fiji
FIJI_DIR=/Fiji.app
FIJI="$FIJI_DIR/ImageJ-linux64"
# Update all components that the Updater would select by default
"$FIJI" --update update || true

echo "Fiji/BDV ecosystem updated."

toolCallRounds.response:
**Findings**
- There is a real script bug at [environment/postInstall](environment/postInstall#L121): `cd comb || truecode` (`truecode` is a typo). If `cd comb` fails, the script exits because of `set -eu`.
- Extension installs are allowed to fail silently at [environment/postInstall](environment/postInstall#L72-L73) due to `|| true`, so Python/Jupyter extension failure is hidden.
- The script installs/extensions under a custom profile (`--user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data`) at [environment/postInstall](environment/postInstall#L72-L73), but VS Code launched from desktop may use the default profile. That profile mismatch can cause “no kernel detected.”
- The alias added at [environment/postInstall](environment/postInstall#L80) can also create inconsistent behavior depending on launch method (terminal alias vs GUI launcher).
- There is no explicit kernel registration step (e.g. `python3 -m ipykernel install --sys-prefix --name python3`) after installs, so kernel discovery depends on auto-detection.

**Most likely why kernel isn’t detected**
- Kernel detection is failing because VS Code is running in a different profile/interpreter context than where extensions/packages were installed, and extension install errors are being suppressed.

Want me to patch `postInstall` to fix the typo, stop swallowing extension install errors, and add explicit kernel registration + consistent profile handling?

**Findings**
- There is a real script bug at

: `cd comb || truecode` (`truecode` is a typo). If `cd comb` fails, the script exits because of `set -eu`.
- Extension installs are allowed to fail silently at

due to `|| true`, so Python/Jupyter extension failure is hidden.
- The script installs/extensions under a custom profile (`--user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data`) at

, but VS Code launched from desktop may use the default profile. That profile mismatch can cause “no kernel detected.”
- The alias added at

can also create inconsistent behavior depending on launch method (terminal alias vs GUI launcher).
- There is no explicit kernel registration step (e.g. `python3 -m ipykernel install --sys-prefix --name python3`) after installs, so kernel discovery depends on auto-detection.

**Most likely why kernel isn’t detected**
- Kernel detection is failing because VS Code is running in a different profile/interpreter context than where extensions/packages were installed, and extension install errors are being suppressed.

Want me to patch
to fix the typo, stop swallowing extension install errors, and add explicit kernel registration + consistent profile handling?

<attachments>
<attachment id="postInstall" filePath="/root/capsule/environment/postInstall">
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu

echo "==== Installing Visual Studio Code (.deb) ===="

TMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"' EXIT
cd "$TMP_DIR"

fetch() {
  URL="$1"
  OUT="$2"
  if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then  
    curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
  elif command -v wget >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    wget -q "$URL" -O "$OUT"
  else
    if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "curl/wget not found; installing curl..."
      apt-get update
      apt-get install -y curl
      curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
    else
      echo "Error: neither curl nor wget is available, and no apt-get to install them."
      exit 127
    fi
  fi
}

echo "Downloading VS Code .deb package..."
fetch "https://code.visualstudio.com/sha/download?build=stable&os=linux-deb-x64" "vscode.deb"

if ! command -v dpkg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Error: dpkg not found. This script supports Debian/Ubuntu images only."
  exit 1
fi

echo "Installing VS Code..."
# Try install; if deps missing, fix them and retry
if ! dpkg -i vscode.deb 2>/dev/null; then
  if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    apt-get update
    apt-get install -y -f
    dpkg -i vscode.deb
  else
    echo "Error: apt-get not available to resolve dependencies."
    exit 1
  fi
fi

# Simple, build-safe verification (no GUI/sandbox)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1 && dpkg -s code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "VS Code installed successfully:"
  dpkg -s code | awk -F': ' '/^Version:/{print "Version:", $2}'
else
  echo "Error: VS Code installation failed."
  exit 1
fi

# Ensure the folder exists
mkdir -p /root/capsule/code/vscode-data

# Upgrade pip and install Jupyter
echo "Installing/Upgrading pip and Jupyter..."
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip
python3 -m pip install jupyter

# Install VS Code extensions for Python + Jupyter
# (These will go into /root/.vscode-server/extensions or ~/.vscode/extensions)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Installing VS Code Python and Jupyter extensions..."
  code --install-extension ms-python.python --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
  code --install-extension ms-toolsai.jupyter --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
else
  echo "Warning: 'code' command not found — skipping VS Code extension installation."
fi

# Create a system-wide alias for all future shells
# now you can simply run 'code' in Terminal
echo "alias code='code --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox'" >> /etc/bash.bashrc

# installing utilities and cleaning apt caches
apt-get update
apt-get install -y numlockx gthumb
rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
apt-get clean
rm -rf /var/cache/apt/archives/*

# ensure pip tooling is up-to-date
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel

python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
  pandas \
  seaborn \
  matplotlib \
  scipy \
  tifffile \
  zarr \
  "numpy==1.26.4" \
  "opencv-python-headless==4.11.0.86" \
  codeocean==0.14.0 \
  aind_session \
  pytest \
  pillow \
  pandas \
  pyyaml \
  aind-ophys-utils \
  pymongo \
  ipykernel \
  scikit-learn \
  suite2p \
  pyqtgraph \
  pyqt5 \
  'napari[all]' \
  'pydantic<2.11' \
  plotly \
  anywidget \
  ipympl

echo "Registering Python kernel for Jupyter..."
python3 -m ipykernel install --sys-prefix --name python3 --display-name "Python 3"
python3 -m jupyter kernelspec list

cd /
git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/comb
cd comb
pip install -e .
#git checkout 21b9cae9431f7f83e1973af6f1fc1ecd40fc765d
git checkout for_gcamp_validation

cd /
git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/lamf-analysis
cd lamf-analysis
pip install -e .

# Update Fiji
FIJI_DIR=/Fiji.app
FIJI="$FIJI_DIR/ImageJ-linux64"
# Update all components that the Updater would select by default
"$FIJI" --update update

echo "Fiji/BDV ecosystem updated."
</attachment>

toolCallRounds.response:
Short answer: **unlikely VS Code version**. Your VS Code is current at [1-Jupyter.log](code/vscode-data/logs/20260226T220137/window1/exthost/output_logging_20260226T220326/1-Jupyter.log#L1), and the failure is in Python environment discovery timing out.

- The Python env locator repeatedly times out at [Python Environments.log](code/vscode-data/logs/20260226T220137/window1/exthost/ms-python.vscode-python-envs/Python%20Environments.log#L30) and [Python Environments.log](code/vscode-data/logs/20260226T220137/window1/exthost/ms-python.vscode-python-envs/Python%20Environments.log#L212).
- It still resolves `/opt/conda/bin/python` at [Python Environments.log](code/vscode-data/logs/20260226T220137/window1/exthost/ms-python.vscode-python-envs/Python%20Environments.log#L134), but activation fails due the timeout at [Python.log](code/vscode-data/logs/20260226T220137/window1/exthost/ms-python.python/Python.log#L39).
- Jupyter then reports interpreter refresh failure at [1-Jupyter.log](code/vscode-data/logs/20260226T220137/window1/exthost/output_logging_20260226T220326/1-Jupyter.log#L10).

Most likely trigger is workspace scale (`/root/capsule` includes very large `data/` trees), not missing installs.

Quick workaround now:
- Run **Python: Select Interpreter** → **Enter interpreter path** → `/opt/conda/bin/python`.
- Or open only `/root/capsule/code` as workspace (smaller tree) and retry kernel selection.

If you want, I can patch [environment/postInstall](environment/postInstall) to auto-create `.vscode/settings.json` with a fixed interpreter path + excludes for large folders so this stops happening on fresh capsules.

thinking.text: **Proposing quick manual workaround with settings patch offer**

v.value: Short answer: **unlikely VS Code version**. Your VS Code is current at

, and the failure is in Python environment discovery timing out.

- The Python env locator repeatedly times out at
, but activation fails due the timeout at

.
- Jupyter then reports interpreter refresh failure at

.

Most likely trigger is workspace scale (

trees), not missing installs.

Quick workaround now:
- Run **Python: Select Interpreter** → **Enter interpreter path** →

as workspace (smaller tree) and retry kernel selection.

If you want, I can patch
with a fixed interpreter path + excludes for large folders so this stops happening on fresh capsules.

renderedUserMessage.text:
<notebookFormatInstructions>
When generating notebook content, use a JSON format. 
1. Each cell must be a valid JSON object within the cells array property with a `metadata.language` property indicating the type of content (e.g., `markdown`, `python`). 
2. Existing cells must contain the `metadata.id` property to uniquely identify each cell. 
3. New cells do not need a `metadata.id` property. 
4. Ensure the content is valid JSON and logically structured. 
5. Do not reference Cell Ids (as users cannot see these values) in user messages, instead use the Cell number (starting from 1).

            "source": [
                "# Purpose:",
                "- Running another round of landmark extension.",
                "- Based on the 1st iteration, where no GFP threshold was used.",
                "    - Now set the threshold based on QC'ed ROIs.",
                "    - Now simply use distance matrix. Don't iterate, make one matrix, and match all at once.",
                "        - This reduces mismatching between nearby cells.",
                "- Metrics:",
                "    - How many more co-registered cells after another iteration?",
                "    - How much does additional manual step help?",
                "# Protocol:",
                "- Filter HCR data based on spot counts and density",
                "- Find nearest matches (greedy method)",
                "",
                "# Update: 11/18/2025 JK",
                "- Generic iteration (2nd ~ )",
                "- Choose between nearest point (after filtering) or finding max HCR value (either count or density) within the neighborhood.",
                "",
                ""
            ]
        },
        {
            "cell_type": "code",

            "metadata": {

            "source": [
                "import os",
                "import zarr",
                "import numpy as np",

            "cell_type": "code",

            "metadata": {

            },
            "source": [
                "def get_ids_from_landmarks(landmarks):",
                "    columns = ['ids', 'active', 'czstack_x', 'czstack_y', 'czstack_z', 'hcr_x', 'hcr_y', 'hcr_z']",
                "    assert len(landmarks.columns) == len(columns)",
                "    if not all([a==b for a,b in zip(landmarks.columns, columns)]):",
                "        landmarks.columns = columns",
                "    matched_ids = landmarks['ids'].values",
                "    def _get_ids(x):",
                "        if x.startswith('cz'):",
                "            cz_id = int(x.split('-')[0].split('cz')[-1])",
                "            hcr_id = int(x.split('-')[1].split('hcr')[-1])",
                "            return cz_id, hcr_id",
                "        else:",
                "            return -1",
                "    ",
                "    ids = [_get_ids(x) for x in matched_ids]",
                "    ids = [id for id in ids if id != -1]",
                "    zstack_ids = [id[0] for id in ids]",
                "    hcr_ids = [id[1] for id in ids]",
                "    return zstack_ids, hcr_ids"
            ]
        },
        {
            "cell_type": "code",
            "id": "#VSC-ddf700b2",
            "metadata": {
                "language": "python"
            },
            "source": [
                "def choose_max_count_nearest_neighbor(HCR_centroids_est, leftover_HCR_df, ",
                "                                      feature='density', k=5, resolve_duplicates=True):",
                "    \"\"\" Choose nearest neighbor with maximum spot counts among k nearest candidates.",
                "    If resolve_duplicates is True, attempt to resolve many-to-one matches by reassigning duplicates",
                "",
                "    Parameters:",
                "    -----------",
                "    HCR_centroids_est : ndarray of shape (M, 3)",
                "        Estimated HCR centroids from leftover czstack centroids",
                "    leftover_HCR_df : DataFrame of shape (N, 3)",
                "        DataFrame containing leftover HCR centroids with columns ['hcr_x', 'hcr_y', 'hcr_z']",
                "    feature : str",
                "        Feature to use for matching. 'count' or 'density'",
                "    k : int",
                "        Number of nearest neighbors to consider",
                "    resolve_duplicates : bool",
                "        Whether to attempt to resolve many-to-one matches",
                "    Returns:",
                "    --------",
                "    chosen_indices : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Index of matched target point for each source point (-1 if unmatched)",
                "    chosen_distances : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Distance to matched target point for each source point (inf if unmatched)",
                "    dist_matrix : ndarray of shape (M, N)",
                "        Pairwise distance matrix between source and target points",
                "    resolve_duplicates : bool",
                "        Whether to attempt to resolve many-to-one matches",
                "    Returns:",
                "    --------",
                "    chosen_indices : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Index of matched target point for each source point (-1 if unmatched)",
                "    chosen_distances : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Distance to matched target point for each source point (inf if unmatched)",
                "    dist_matrix : ndarray of shape (M, N)",
                "        Pairwise distance matrix between source and target points",
                "    \"\"\"",
                "    hcr_centroids = leftover_HCR_df[['hcr_x','hcr_y','hcr_z']].to_numpy()",
                "    counts = leftover_HCR_df[feature].to_numpy()",
                "",
                "    dist_matrix = distance_matrix(HCR_centroids_est, hcr_centroids)",
                "    k = min(k, dist_matrix.shape[1])",
                "",
                "    # Indices of k smallest distances per source (row)",
                "    nearest_indices = np.argpartition(dist_matrix, k, axis=1)[:, :k]",
                "    nearest_distances = np.take_along_axis(dist_matrix, nearest_indices, axis=1)",
                "",
                "    # Counts for those candidates",
                "    nearest_counts = counts[nearest_indices]",
                "",
                "    # Pick candidate with max counts per row",
                "    best_local_idx = np.argmax(nearest_counts, axis=1)",
                "    row_idx = np.arange(nearest_indices.shape[0])",
                "    chosen_indices = nearest_indices[row_idx, best_local_idx]",
                "    chosen_distances = nearest_distances[row_idx, best_local_idx]",
                "",
                "    # Detect many-to-one",
                "    # targets mapped multiple times",
                "    _, inverse, counts_per_target = np.unique(chosen_indices, return_inverse=True, return_counts=True)",
                "    duplicate_sources_mask = counts_per_target[inverse] > 1",
                "",
                "    if resolve_duplicates and duplicate_sources_mask.any():",
                "        print(f'Resolving {np.sum(duplicate_sources_mask)} duplicate matches...')",
                "        # Keep only the source with minimal distance for each duplicated target",
                "        # For duplicates: build per-target minimal distance",
                "        keep_mask = np.ones_like(chosen_indices, dtype=bool)",
                "        dup_targets = np.unique(chosen_indices[duplicate_sources_mask])",
                "        for t in dup_targets:",
                "            sel = np.where(chosen_indices == t)[0]",
                "            best = sel[np.argmin(chosen_distances[sel])]",
                "            sel_remove = sel[sel != best]",
                "            keep_mask[sel_remove] = False",
                "",
                "        # Optionally attempt second-best for removed rows (still within their k candidates)",
                "        removed_rows = np.where(~keep_mask)[0]",
                "        if len(removed_rows):",
                "            # For each removed row choose next best (by counts) that is not already taken",
                "            taken = set(chosen_indices[keep_mask])",
                "            # Mask out previously chosen best",
                "            nearest_counts_removed = nearest_counts[removed_rows].copy()",
                "            nearest_counts_removed[np.arange(len(removed_rows)), best_local_idx[removed_rows]] = -1",
                "            # Iterate only over removed rows (small set)",
                "            for j, r in enumerate(removed_rows):",
                "                # Sort candidates by counts descending",
                "                cand_order = np.argsort(-nearest_counts_removed[j])",
                "                for cand_pos in cand_order:",
                "                    cand_target = nearest_indices[r, cand_pos]",
                "                    if cand_target not in taken:",
                "                        chosen_indices[r] = cand_target",
                "                        chosen_distances[r] = nearest_distances[r, cand_pos]",
                "                        taken.add(cand_target)",
                "                        break",
                "                else:",
                "                    # Could not find alternative unique target; mark unmatched",
                "                    chosen_indices[r] = -1",
                "                    chosen_distances[r] = np.inf",
                "",
                "    return chosen_indices, chosen_distances, dist_matrix"
            ]
        },
        {
            "cell_type": "code",
            "id": "#VSC-bf6a61cb",
            "metadata": {
                "language": "python"
            },
            "source": [
                "# One-to-One Matching Algorithm",
                "# Solves the many-to-one problem by iteratively assigning best matches",
                "",
                "def one_to_one_matching(HCR_centroids_est, leftover_HCR_df):",
                "    \"\"\"",
                "    Solve one-to-one matching from distance matrix.",
                "    ",
                "    Parameters:",
                "    -----------",
                "    HCR_centroids_est : ndarray of shape (M, 3)",
                "        Estimated HCR centroids from leftover czstack centroids",
                "    leftover_HCR_df : DataFrame of shape (N, 3)",
                "        DataFrame containing leftover HCR centroids with columns ['hcr_x', 'hcr_y', 'hcr_z']",
                "    ",
                "    Returns:",
                "    --------",
                "    matched_indices : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Index of matched target point for each source point (-1 if unmatched)",
                "    matched_distances : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Distance to matched target point for each source point (inf if unmatched)",
                "    dist_matrix : ndarray of shape (M, N)",
                "        Pairwise distance matrix between source and target points",
                "    \"\"\"",
                "    dist_matrix = distance_matrix(HCR_centroids_est, leftover_HCR_df[['hcr_x','hcr_y','hcr_z']].values)",
                "    M, N = dist_matrix.shape",
                "    ",
                "    # Initialize output arrays",
                "    matched_indices = np.full(M, -1, dtype=int)",
                "    matched_distances = np.full(M, np.inf)",
                "    ",
                "    # Keep track of which target points are already assigned",
                "    used_targets = np.zeros(N, dtype=bool)",
                "    ",
                "    # Create a copy of distance matrix that we can modify",
                "    working_dist_matrix = dist_matrix.copy()",
                "    ",
                "    # Process matches iteratively",
                "    for iteration in range(min(M, N)):",
                "        # Find the global minimum in the remaining matrix",
                "        flat_idx = np.argmin(working_dist_matrix)",
                "        source_idx, target_idx = np.unravel_index(flat_idx, working_dist_matrix.shape)",
                "        ",
                "        # Record this match",
                "        matched_indices[source_idx] = target_idx",
                "        matched_distances[source_idx] = working_dist_matrix[source_idx, target_idx]",
                "        ",
                "        # Mark this target as used",
                "        used_targets[target_idx] = True",
                "        ",
                "        # Remove this source row and target column from consideration",
                "        working_dist_matrix[source_idx, :] = np.inf",
                "        working_dist_matrix[:, target_idx] = np.inf",
                "    ",
                "    return matched_indices, matched_distances, dist_matrix",
                "",
                ""
            ]
        },

edits.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu

echo "==== Installing Visual Studio Code (.deb) ===="

TMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"' EXIT
cd "$TMP_DIR"

fetch() {
  URL="$1"
  OUT="$2"
  if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then  
    curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
  elif command -v wget >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    wget -q "$URL" -O "$OUT"
  else
    if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "curl/wget not found; installing curl..."
      apt-get update
      apt-get install -y curl
      curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
    else
      echo "Error: neither curl nor wget is available, and no apt-get to install them."
      exit 127
    fi
  fi
}

echo "Downloading VS Code .deb package..."
fetch "https://code.visualstudio.com/sha/download?build=stable&os=linux-deb-x64" "vscode.deb"

if ! command -v dpkg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Error: dpkg not found. This script supports Debian/Ubuntu images only."
  exit 1
fi

echo "Installing VS Code..."
# Try install; if deps missing, fix them and retry
if ! dpkg -i vscode.deb 2>/dev/null; then
  if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    apt-get update
    apt-get install -y -f
    dpkg -i vscode.deb
  else
    echo "Error: apt-get not available to resolve dependencies."
    exit 1
  fi
fi

# Simple, build-safe verification (no GUI/sandbox)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1 && dpkg -s code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "VS Code installed successfully:"
  dpkg -s code | awk -F': ' '/^Version:/{print "Version:", $2}'
else
  echo "Error: VS Code installation failed."
  exit 1
fi

# Ensure the folder exists
mkdir -p /root/capsule/code/vscode-data

# Upgrade pip and install Jupyter
echo "Installing/Upgrading pip and Jupyter..."
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip
python3 -m pip install jupyter

# Install VS Code extensions for Python + Jupyter
# (These will go into /root/.vscode-server/extensions or ~/.vscode/extensions)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Installing VS Code Python and Jupyter extensions..."
  code --install-extension ms-python.python --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
  code --install-extension ms-toolsai.jupyter --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
else
  echo "Warning: 'code' command not found — skipping VS Code extension installation."
fi

# Create a system-wide alias for all future shells
# now you can simply run 'code' in Terminal
echo "alias code='code --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox'" >> /etc/bash.bashrc

# installing utilities and cleaning apt caches
apt-get update
apt-get install -y numlockx gthumb
rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
apt-get clean
rm -rf /var/cache/apt/archives/*

# ensure pip tooling is up-to-date
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel

python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
  pandas \
  seaborn \
  matplotlib \
  scipy \
  tifffile \
  zarr \
  "numpy==1.26.4" \
  "opencv-python-headless==4.11.0.86" \
  codeocean==0.14.0 \
  aind_session \
  pytest \
  pillow \
  pandas \
  pyyaml \
  aind-ophys-utils \
  pymongo \
  ipykernel \
  scikit-learn \
  suite2p \
  pyqtgraph \
  pyqt5 \
  'napari[all]' \
  'pydantic<2.11' \
  plotly \
  anywidget \
  ipympl

echo "Registering Python kernel for Jupyter..."
python3 -m ipykernel install --sys-prefix --name python3 --display-name "Python 3"
python3 -m jupyter kernelspec list

echo "Configuring workspace VS Code settings for stable Python/Jupyter detection..."
mkdir -p /root/capsule/.vscode
cat > /root/capsule/.vscode/settings.json << 'EOF'
{
  "python.defaultInterpreterPath": "/opt/conda/bin/python",
  "jupyter.jupyterServerType": "local",
  "python.terminal.activateEnvironment": true,
  "files.watcherExclude": {
    "**/.git/**": true,
    "**/data/**": true,
    "**/results/**": true,
    "**/scratch/**": true
  },
  "search.exclude": {
    "**/data/**": true,
    "**/results/**": true,
    "**/scratch/**": true
  }
}
EOF

cd /
git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/comb
cd comb
pip install -e .
#git checkout 21b9cae9431f7f83e1973af6f1fc1ecd40fc765d
git checkout for_gcamp_validation

cd /
git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/lamf-analysis
cd lamf-analysis
pip install -e .

# Update Fiji
FIJI_DIR=/Fiji.app
FIJI="$FIJI_DIR/ImageJ-linux64"
# Update all components that the Updater would select by default
"$FIJI" --update update

echo "Fiji/BDV ecosystem updated."

            "source": [
                "def get_ids_from_landmarks(landmarks):",
                "    columns = ['ids', 'active', 'czstack_x', 'czstack_y', 'czstack_z', 'hcr_x', 'hcr_y', 'hcr_z']",
                "    assert len(landmarks.columns) == len(columns)",
                "    if not all([a==b for a,b in zip(landmarks.columns, columns)]):",
                "        landmarks.columns = columns",
                "    matched_ids = landmarks['ids'].values",
                "    def _get_ids(x):",
                "        if x.startswith('cz'):",
                "            cz_id = int(x.split('-')[0].split('cz')[-1])",
                "            hcr_id = int(x.split('-')[1].split('hcr')[-1])",
                "            return cz_id, hcr_id",
                "        else:",
                "            return -1",
                "    ",
                "    ids = [_get_ids(x) for x in matched_ids]",
                "    ids = [id for id in ids if id != -1]",
                "    zstack_ids = [id[0] for id in ids]",
                "    hcr_ids = [id[1] for id in ids]",
                "    return zstack_ids, hcr_ids"
            ]
        },
        {
            "cell_type": "code",
            "id": "#VSC-ddf700b2",
            "metadata": {
                "language": "python"
            },
            "source": [
                "def choose_max_count_nearest_neighbor(HCR_centroids_est, leftover_HCR_df, ",
                "                                      feature='density', k=5, resolve_duplicates=True):",
                "    \"\"\" Choose nearest neighbor with maximum spot counts among k nearest candidates.",
                "    If resolve_duplicates is True, attempt to resolve many-to-one matches by reassigning duplicates",
                "",
                "    Parameters:",
                "    -----------",
                "    HCR_centroids_est : ndarray of shape (M, 3)",
                "        Estimated HCR centroids from leftover czstack centroids",
                "    leftover_HCR_df : DataFrame of shape (N, 3)",
                "        DataFrame containing leftover HCR centroids with columns ['hcr_x', 'hcr_y', 'hcr_z']",
                "    feature : str",
                "        Feature to use for matching. 'count' or 'density'",
                "    k : int",
                "        Number of nearest neighbors to consider",
                "    resolve_duplicates : bool",
                "        Whether to attempt to resolve many-to-one matches",
                "    Returns:",
                "    --------",
                "    chosen_indices : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Index of matched target point for each source point (-1 if unmatched)",
                "    chosen_distances : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Distance to matched target point for each source point (inf if unmatched)",
                "    dist_matrix : ndarray of shape (M, N)",
                "        Pairwise distance matrix between source and target points",
                "    resolve_duplicates : bool",
                "        Whether to attempt to resolve many-to-one matches",
                "    Returns:",
                "    --------",
                "    chosen_indices : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Index of matched target point for each source point (-1 if unmatched)",
                "    chosen_distances : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Distance to matched target point for each source point (inf if unmatched)",
                "    dist_matrix : ndarray of shape (M, N)",
                "        Pairwise distance matrix between source and target points",
                "    \"\"\"",
                "    hcr_centroids = leftover_HCR_df[['hcr_x','hcr_y','hcr_z']].to_numpy()",
                "    counts = leftover_HCR_df[feature].to_numpy()",
                "",
                "    dist_matrix = distance_matrix(HCR_centroids_est, hcr_centroids)",
                "    k = min(k, dist_matrix.shape[1])",
                "",
                "    # Indices of k smallest distances per source (row)",
                "    nearest_indices = np.argpartition(dist_matrix, k, axis=1)[:, :k]",
                "    nearest_distances = np.take_along_axis(dist_matrix, nearest_indices, axis=1)",
                "",
                "    # Counts for those candidates",
                "    nearest_counts = counts[nearest_indices]",
                "",
                "    # Pick candidate with max counts per row",
                "    best_local_idx = np.argmax(nearest_counts, axis=1)",
                "    row_idx = np.arange(nearest_indices.shape[0])",
                "    chosen_indices = nearest_indices[row_idx, best_local_idx]",
                "    chosen_distances = nearest_distances[row_idx, best_local_idx]",
                "",
                "    # Detect many-to-one",
                "    # targets mapped multiple times",
                "    _, inverse, counts_per_target = np.unique(chosen_indices, return_inverse=True, return_counts=True)",
                "    duplicate_sources_mask = counts_per_target[inverse] > 1",
                "",
                "    if resolve_duplicates and duplicate_sources_mask.any():",
                "        print(f'Resolving {np.sum(duplicate_sources_mask)} duplicate matches...')",
                "        # Keep only the source with minimal distance for each duplicated target",
                "        # For duplicates: build per-target minimal distance",
                "        keep_mask = np.ones_like(chosen_indices, dtype=bool)",
                "        dup_targets = np.unique(chosen_indices[duplicate_sources_mask])",
                "        for t in dup_targets:",
                "            sel = np.where(chosen_indices == t)[0]",
                "            best = sel[np.argmin(chosen_distances[sel])]",
                "            sel_remove = sel[sel != best]",
                "            keep_mask[sel_remove] = False",
                "",
                "        # Optionally attempt second-best for removed rows (still within their k candidates)",
                "        removed_rows = np.where(~keep_mask)[0]",
                "        if len(removed_rows):",
                "            # For each removed row choose next best (by counts) that is not already taken",
                "            taken = set(chosen_indices[keep_mask])",
                "            # Mask out previously chosen best",
                "            nearest_counts_removed = nearest_counts[removed_rows].copy()",
                "            nearest_counts_removed[np.arange(len(removed_rows)), best_local_idx[removed_rows]] = -1",
                "            # Iterate only over removed rows (small set)",
                "            for j, r in enumerate(removed_rows):",
                "                # Sort candidates by counts descending",
                "                cand_order = np.argsort(-nearest_counts_removed[j])",
                "                for cand_pos in cand_order:",
                "                    cand_target = nearest_indices[r, cand_pos]",
                "                    if cand_target not in taken:",
                "                        chosen_indices[r] = cand_target",
                "                        chosen_distances[r] = nearest_distances[r, cand_pos]",
                "                        taken.add(cand_target)",
                "                        break",
                "                else:",
                "                    # Could not find alternative unique target; mark unmatched",
                "                    chosen_indices[r] = -1",
                "                    chosen_distances[r] = np.inf",
                "",
                "    return chosen_indices, chosen_distances, dist_matrix"
            ]
        },
        {
            "cell_type": "code",
            "id": "#VSC-bf6a61cb",
            "metadata": {
                "language": "python"
            },
            "source": [
                "# One-to-One Matching Algorithm",
                "# Solves the many-to-one problem by iteratively assigning best matches",
                "",
                "def one_to_one_matching(HCR_centroids_est, leftover_HCR_df):",
                "    \"\"\"",
                "    Solve one-to-one matching from distance matrix.",
                "    ",
                "    Parameters:",
                "    -----------",
                "    HCR_centroids_est : ndarray of shape (M, 3)",
                "        Estimated HCR centroids from leftover czstack centroids",
                "    leftover_HCR_df : DataFrame of shape (N, 3)",
                "        DataFrame containing leftover HCR centroids with columns ['hcr_x', 'hcr_y', 'hcr_z']",
                "    ",
                "    Returns:",
                "    --------",
                "    matched_indices : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Index of matched target point for each source point (-1 if unmatched)",
                "    matched_distances : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Distance to matched target point for each source point (inf if unmatched)",
                "    dist_matrix : ndarray of shape (M, N)",
                "        Pairwise distance matrix between source and target points",
                "    \"\"\"",
                "    dist_matrix = distance_matrix(HCR_centroids_est, leftover_HCR_df[['hcr_x','hcr_y','hcr_z']].values)",
                "    M, N = dist_matrix.shape",
                "    ",
                "    # Initialize output arrays",
                "    matched_indices = np.full(M, -1, dtype=int)",
                "    matched_distances = np.full(M, np.inf)",
                "    ",
                "    # Keep track of which target points are already assigned",
                "    used_targets = np.zeros(N, dtype=bool)",
                "    ",
                "    # Create a copy of distance matrix that we can modify",
                "    working_dist_matrix = dist_matrix.copy()",
                "    ",
                "    # Process matches iteratively",
                "    for iteration in range(min(M, N)):",
                "        # Find the global minimum in the remaining matrix",
                "        flat_idx = np.argmin(working_dist_matrix)",
                "        source_idx, target_idx = np.unravel_index(flat_idx, working_dist_matrix.shape)",
                "        ",
                "        # Record this match",
                "        matched_indices[source_idx] = target_idx",
                "        matched_distances[source_idx] = working_dist_matrix[source_idx, target_idx]",
                "        ",
                "        # Mark this target as used",
                "        used_targets[target_idx] = True",
                "        ",
                "        # Remove this source row and target column from consideration",
                "        working_dist_matrix[source_idx, :] = np.inf",
                "        working_dist_matrix[:, target_idx] = np.inf",
                "    ",
                "    return matched_indices, matched_distances, dist_matrix",
                "",
                ""
            ]
        },

edits.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu

echo "==== Installing Visual Studio Code (.deb) ===="

TMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"' EXIT
cd "$TMP_DIR"

fetch() {
  URL="$1"
  OUT="$2"
  if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then  
    curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
  elif command -v wget >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    wget -q "$URL" -O "$OUT"
  else
    if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "curl/wget not found; installing curl..."
      apt-get update
      apt-get install -y curl
      curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
    else
      echo "Error: neither curl nor wget is available, and no apt-get to install them."
      exit 127
    fi
  fi
}

echo "Downloading VS Code .deb package..."
fetch "https://code.visualstudio.com/sha/download?build=stable&os=linux-deb-x64" "vscode.deb"

if ! command -v dpkg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Error: dpkg not found. This script supports Debian/Ubuntu images only."
  exit 1
fi

echo "Installing VS Code..."
# Try install; if deps missing, fix them and retry
if ! dpkg -i vscode.deb 2>/dev/null; then
  if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    apt-get update
    apt-get install -y -f
    dpkg -i vscode.deb
  else
    echo "Error: apt-get not available to resolve dependencies."
    exit 1
  fi
fi

# Simple, build-safe verification (no GUI/sandbox)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1 && dpkg -s code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "VS Code installed successfully:"
  dpkg -s code | awk -F': ' '/^Version:/{print "Version:", $2}'
else
  echo "Error: VS Code installation failed."
  exit 1
fi

# Ensure the folder exists
mkdir -p /root/capsule/code/vscode-data

# Upgrade pip and install Jupyter
echo "Installing/Upgrading pip and Jupyter..."
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip
python3 -m pip install jupyter

# Install VS Code extensions for Python + Jupyter
# (These will go into /root/.vscode-server/extensions or ~/.vscode/extensions)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Installing VS Code Python and Jupyter extensions..."
  code --install-extension ms-python.python --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
  code --install-extension ms-toolsai.jupyter --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
else
  echo "Warning: 'code' command not found — skipping VS Code extension installation."
fi

# Create a system-wide alias for all future shells
# now you can simply run 'code' in Terminal
echo "alias code='code --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox'" >> /etc/bash.bashrc

# installing utilities and cleaning apt caches
apt-get update
apt-get install -y numlockx gthumb
rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
apt-get clean
rm -rf /var/cache/apt/archives/*

# ensure pip tooling is up-to-date
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel

python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
  pandas \
  seaborn \
  matplotlib \
  scipy \
  tifffile \
  zarr \
  "numpy==1.26.4" \
  "opencv-python-headless==4.11.0.86" \
  codeocean==0.14.0 \
  aind_session \
  pytest \
  pillow \
  pandas \
  pyyaml \
  aind-ophys-utils \
  pymongo \
  ipykernel \
  scikit-learn \
  suite2p \
  pyqtgraph \
  pyqt5 \
  'napari[all]' \
  'pydantic<2.11' \
  plotly \
  anywidget \
  ipympl

echo "Registering Python kernel for Jupyter..."
python3 -m ipykernel install --sys-prefix --name python3 --display-name "Python 3"
python3 -m jupyter kernelspec list

echo "Configuring workspace VS Code settings for stable Python/Jupyter detection..."
mkdir -p /root/capsule/.vscode
cat > /root/capsule/.vscode/settings.json << 'EOF'
{
  "python-envs.defaultEnvManager": "ms-python.python:venv",
  "python-envs.defaultPackageManager": "ms-python.python:pip",
  "python.defaultInterpreterPath": "/opt/conda/bin/python",
  "python.condaPath": "/opt/conda/bin/conda",
  "jupyter.jupyterServerType": "local",
  "jupyter.kernels.excludePythonEnvironments": [],
  "python.terminal.activateEnvironment": true,
  "files.watcherExclude": {
    "**/.git/**": true,
    "**/data/**": true,
    "**/results/**": true,
    "**/scratch/**": true
  },
  "search.exclude": {
    "**/data/**": true,
    "**/results/**": true,
    "**/scratch/**": true
  }
}
EOF

cd /
git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/comb
cd comb
pip install -e .
#git checkout 21b9cae9431f7f83e1973af6f1fc1ecd40fc765d
git checkout for_gcamp_validation

cd /
git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/lamf-analysis
cd lamf-analysis
pip install -e .

# Update Fiji
FIJI_DIR=/Fiji.app
FIJI="$FIJI_DIR/ImageJ-linux64"
# Update all components that the Updater would select by default
"$FIJI" --update update

echo "Fiji/BDV ecosystem updated."

v.generatedTitle: Updated settings.json and checked related files

v.value:
Exactly—kernel metadata in one notebook is per-file.  
I’ve now made the behavior general at workspace/bootstrap level:

- Updated

so all notebooks use the same interpreter automatically:
  - `python.defaultInterpreterPath: /opt/conda/bin/python`
  - `jupyter.jupyterServerType: local`
  - `python.condaPath: /opt/conda/bin/conda`
  - switched `python-envs.defaultEnvManager`/package manager away from conda to reduce timeout-prone conda discovery.
- Updated

to generate the same settings on every new capsule restart.

This is now workspace-wide, not tied to a specific notebook.  
Run `Developer: Reload Window` once; after that, newly opened notebooks should auto-pick the Python kernel without `Python: Select Interpreter`.

Want me to also remove the notebook-specific kernel metadata from
so everything relies purely on global settings?

                "import os",
                "import zarr",
                "import numpy as np",
                "import tifffile as tiff",

                "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt",
                "from tqdm import tqdm",
                "from scipy.interpolate import Rbf",
                "from scipy.spatial import distance_matrix",
                "import cv2",
                "import os",
                "import json",
                "from pathlib import Path",
                "",
                "DATA_DIR = Path('/root/capsule/data/')",
                "",
                "%load_ext autoreload",
                "%autoreload 2"
            ]
        },
        {
            "cell_type": "code",
            "id": "#VSC-aef5dbeb",
            "metadata": {
                "language": "python"
            },
            "source": [
                "def get_ids_from_landmarks(landmarks):",
                "    columns = ['ids', 'active', 'czstack_x', 'czstack_y', 'czstack_z', 'hcr_x', 'hcr_y', 'hcr_z']",
                "    assert len(landmarks.columns) == len(columns)",
                "    if not all([a==b for a,b in zip(landmarks.columns, columns)]):",
                "        landmarks.columns = columns",
                "    matched_ids = landmarks['ids'].values",
                "    def _get_ids(x):",
                "        if x.startswith('cz'):",
                "            cz_id = int(x.split('-')[0].split('cz')[-1])",
                "            hcr_id = int(x.split('-')[1].split('hcr')[-1])",
                "            return cz_id, hcr_id",
                "        else:",
                "            return -1",
                "    ",
                "    ids = [_get_ids(x) for x in matched_ids]",
                "    ids = [id for id in ids if id != -1]",
                "    zstack_ids = [id[0] for id in ids]",
                "    hcr_ids = [id[1] for id in ids]",
                "    return zstack_ids, hcr_ids"
            ]
        },
        {
            "cell_type": "code",
            "id": "#VSC-ddf700b2",
            "metadata": {
                "language": "python"
            },
            "source": [
                "def choose_max_count_nearest_neighbor(HCR_centroids_est, leftover_HCR_df, ",
                "                                      feature='density', k=5, resolve_duplicates=True):",
                "    \"\"\" Choose nearest neighbor with maximum spot counts among k nearest candidates.",
                "    If resolve_duplicates is True, attempt to resolve many-to-one matches by reassigning duplicates",
                "",
                "    Parameters:",
                "    -----------",
                "    HCR_centroids_est : ndarray of shape (M, 3)",
                "        Estimated HCR centroids from leftover czstack centroids",
                "    leftover_HCR_df : DataFrame of shape (N, 3)",
                "        DataFrame containing leftover HCR centroids with columns ['hcr_x', 'hcr_y', 'hcr_z']",
                "    feature : str",
                "        Feature to use for matching. 'count' or 'density'",
                "    k : int",
                "        Number of nearest neighbors to consider",
                "    resolve_duplicates : bool",
                "        Whether to attempt to resolve many-to-one matches",
                "    Returns:",
                "    --------",
                "    chosen_indices : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Index of matched target point for each source point (-1 if unmatched)",
                "    chosen_distances : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Distance to matched target point for each source point (inf if unmatched)",
                "    dist_matrix : ndarray of shape (M, N)",
                "        Pairwise distance matrix between source and target points",
                "    resolve_duplicates : bool",
                "        Whether to attempt to resolve many-to-one matches",
                "    Returns:",
                "    --------",
                "    chosen_indices : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Index of matched target point for each source point (-1 if unmatched)",
                "    chosen_distances : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Distance to matched target point for each source point (inf if unmatched)",
                "    dist_matrix : ndarray of shape (M, N)",
                "        Pairwise distance matrix between source and target points",
                "    \"\"\"",
                "    hcr_centroids = leftover_HCR_df[['hcr_x','hcr_y','hcr_z']].to_numpy()",
                "    counts = leftover_HCR_df[feature].to_numpy()",
                "",
                "    dist_matrix = distance_matrix(HCR_centroids_est, hcr_centroids)",
                "    k = min(k, dist_matrix.shape[1])",
                "",
                "    # Indices of k smallest distances per source (row)",
                "    nearest_indices = np.argpartition(dist_matrix, k, axis=1)[:, :k]",
                "    nearest_distances = np.take_along_axis(dist_matrix, nearest_indices, axis=1)",
                "",
                "    # Counts for those candidates",
                "    nearest_counts = counts[nearest_indices]",
                "",
                "    # Pick candidate with max counts per row",
                "    best_local_idx = np.argmax(nearest_counts, axis=1)",
                "    row_idx = np.arange(nearest_indices.shape[0])",
                "    chosen_indices = nearest_indices[row_idx, best_local_idx]",
                "    chosen_distances = nearest_distances[row_idx, best_local_idx]",
                "",
                "    # Detect many-to-one",
                "    # targets mapped multiple times",
                "    _, inverse, counts_per_target = np.unique(chosen_indices, return_inverse=True, return_counts=True)",
                "    duplicate_sources_mask = counts_per_target[inverse] > 1",
                "",
                "    if resolve_duplicates and duplicate_sources_mask.any():",
                "        print(f'Resolving {np.sum(duplicate_sources_mask)} duplicate matches...')",
                "        # Keep only the source with minimal distance for each duplicated target",
                "        # For duplicates: build per-target minimal distance",
                "        keep_mask = np.ones_like(chosen_indices, dtype=bool)",
                "        dup_targets = np.unique(chosen_indices[duplicate_sources_mask])",
                "        for t in dup_targets:",
                "            sel = np.where(chosen_indices == t)[0]",
                "            best = sel[np.argmin(chosen_distances[sel])]",
                "            sel_remove = sel[sel != best]",
                "            keep_mask[sel_remove] = False",
                "",
                "        # Optionally attempt second-best for removed rows (still within their k candidates)",
                "        removed_rows = np.where(~keep_mask)[0]",
                "        if len(removed_rows):",
                "            # For each removed row choose next best (by counts) that is not already taken",
                "            taken = set(chosen_indices[keep_mask])",
                "            # Mask out previously chosen best",
                "            nearest_counts_removed = nearest_counts[removed_rows].copy()",
                "            nearest_counts_removed[np.arange(len(removed_rows)), best_local_idx[removed_rows]] = -1",
                "            # Iterate only over removed rows (small set)",
                "            for j, r in enumerate(removed_rows):",
                "                # Sort candidates by counts descending",
                "                cand_order = np.argsort(-nearest_counts_removed[j])",
                "                for cand_pos in cand_order:",
                "                    cand_target = nearest_indices[r, cand_pos]",
                "                    if cand_target not in taken:",
                "                        chosen_indices[r] = cand_target",
                "                        chosen_distances[r] = nearest_distances[r, cand_pos]",
                "                        taken.add(cand_target)",
                "                        break",
                "                else:",
                "                    # Could not find alternative unique target; mark unmatched",
                "                    chosen_indices[r] = -1",
                "                    chosen_distances[r] = np.inf",
                "",
                "    return chosen_indices, chosen_distances, dist_matrix"
            ]
        },
        {
            "cell_type": "code",
            "id": "#VSC-bf6a61cb",
            "metadata": {
                "language": "python"
            },
            "source": [
                "# One-to-One Matching Algorithm",
                "# Solves the many-to-one problem by iteratively assigning best matches",
                "",
                "def one_to_one_matching(HCR_centroids_est, leftover_HCR_df):",
                "    \"\"\"",
                "    Solve one-to-one matching from distance matrix.",
                "    ",
                "    Parameters:",
                "    -----------",
                "    HCR_centroids_est : ndarray of shape (M, 3)",
                "        Estimated HCR centroids from leftover czstack centroids",
                "    leftover_HCR_df : DataFrame of shape (N, 3)",
                "        DataFrame containing leftover HCR centroids with columns ['hcr_x', 'hcr_y', 'hcr_z']",
                "    ",
                "    Returns:",
                "    --------",
                "    matched_indices : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Index of matched target point for each source point (-1 if unmatched)",
                "    matched_distances : ndarray of shape (M,)",
                "        Distance to matched target point for each source point (inf if unmatched)",
                "    dist_matrix : ndarray of shape (M, N)",
                "        Pairwise distance matrix between source and target points",
                "    \"\"\"",
                "    dist_matrix = distance_matrix(HCR_centroids_est, leftover_HCR_df[['hcr_x','hcr_y','hcr_z']].values)",
                "    M, N = dist_matrix.shape",
                "    ",
                "    # Initialize output arrays",
                "    matched_indices = np.full(M, -1, dtype=int)",
                "    matched_distances = np.full(M, np.inf)",
                "    ",
                "    # Keep track of which target points are already assigned",
                "    used_targets = np.zeros(N, dtype=bool)",
                "    ",
                "    # Create a copy of distance matrix that we can modify",
                "    working_dist_matrix = dist_matrix.copy()",
                "    ",
                "    # Process matches iteratively",
                "    for iteration in range(min(M, N)):",
                "        # Find the global minimum in the remaining matrix",
                "        flat_idx = np.argmin(working_dist_matrix)",
                "        source_idx, target_idx = np.unravel_index(flat_idx, working_dist_matrix.shape)",
                "        ",
                "        # Record this match",
                "        matched_indices[source_idx] = target_idx",
                "        matched_distances[source_idx] = working_dist_matrix[source_idx, target_idx]",
                "        ",
                "        # Mark this target as used",
                "        used_targets[target_idx] = True",
                "        ",
                "        # Remove this source row and target column from consideration",
                "        working_dist_matrix[source_idx, :] = np.inf",
                "        working_dist_matrix[:, target_idx] = np.inf",
                "    ",
                "    return matched_indices, matched_distances, dist_matrix",
                "",
                ""
            ]
        },

toolCallRounds.response:
**Root Cause**
- The slowdown is from Python Environments (PET) timing out repeatedly, not missing Python itself. The logs show repeated configure timeout loops before kernel list is ready.

**What I changed**
- Disabled the new env-discovery path that is timing out in your workspace: [.vscode/settings.json](.vscode/settings.json)
  - python.useEnvironmentsExtension: false
  - python-envs.workspaceSearchPaths: []
- Applied the same fix to future capsule boots in [environment/postInstall](environment/postInstall).

**Next step (required once)**
- Run Developer: Reload Window.
- Reopen a notebook and click Select Kernel; it should populate much faster using the pinned interpreter path.

If it is still slow after reload, I can apply one more hard fallback: auto-disable the ms-python.vscode-python-envs extension during postInstall.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu

echo "==== Installing Visual Studio Code (.deb) ===="

TMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"' EXIT
cd "$TMP_DIR"

fetch() {
  URL="$1"
  OUT="$2"
  if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then  
    curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
  elif command -v wget >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    wget -q "$URL" -O "$OUT"
  else
    if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "curl/wget not found; installing curl..."
      apt-get update
      apt-get install -y curl
      curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
    else
      echo "Error: neither curl nor wget is available, and no apt-get to install them."
      exit 127
    fi
  fi
}

echo "Downloading VS Code .deb package..."
fetch "https://code.visualstudio.com/sha/download?build=stable&os=linux-deb-x64" "vscode.deb"

if ! command -v dpkg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Error: dpkg not found. This script supports Debian/Ubuntu images only."
  exit 1
fi

echo "Installing VS Code..."
# Try install; if deps missing, fix them and retry
if ! dpkg -i vscode.deb 2>/dev/null; then
  if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    apt-get update
    apt-get install -y -f
    dpkg -i vscode.deb
  else
    echo "Error: apt-get not available to resolve dependencies."
    exit 1
  fi
fi

# Simple, build-safe verification (no GUI/sandbox)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1 && dpkg -s code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "VS Code installed successfully:"
  dpkg -s code | awk -F': ' '/^Version:/{print "Version:", $2}'
else
  echo "Error: VS Code installation failed."
  exit 1
fi

# Ensure the folder exists
mkdir -p /root/capsule/code/vscode-data

# Upgrade pip and install Jupyter
echo "Installing/Upgrading pip and Jupyter..."
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip
python3 -m pip install jupyter

# Install VS Code extensions for Python + Jupyter
# (These will go into /root/.vscode-server/extensions or ~/.vscode/extensions)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Installing VS Code Python and Jupyter extensions..."
  code --install-extension ms-python.python --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
  code --install-extension ms-toolsai.jupyter --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
else
  echo "Warning: 'code' command not found — skipping VS Code extension installation."
fi

# Create a system-wide alias for all future shells
# now you can simply run 'code' in Terminal
echo "alias code='code --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox'" >> /etc/bash.bashrc

# installing utilities and cleaning apt caches
apt-get update
apt-get install -y numlockx gthumb
rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
apt-get clean
rm -rf /var/cache/apt/archives/*

# ensure pip tooling is up-to-date
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel

python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
  pandas \
  seaborn \
  matplotlib \
  scipy \
  tifffile \
  zarr \
  "numpy==1.26.4" \
  "opencv-python-headless==4.11.0.86" \
  codeocean==0.14.0 \
  aind_session \
  pytest \
  pillow \
  pandas \
  pyyaml \
  aind-ophys-utils \
  pymongo \
  ipykernel \
  scikit-learn \
  suite2p \
  pyqtgraph \
  pyqt5 \
  'napari[all]' \
  'pydantic<2.11' \
  plotly \
  anywidget \
  ipympl

echo "Registering Python kernel for Jupyter..."
python3 -m ipykernel install --sys-prefix --name python3 --display-name "Python 3"
python3 -m jupyter kernelspec list

echo "Configuring workspace VS Code settings for stable Python/Jupyter detection..."
mkdir -p /root/capsule/.vscode
cat > /root/capsule/.vscode/settings.json << 'EOF'
{
  "python-envs.defaultEnvManager": "ms-python.python:venv",
  "python-envs.defaultPackageManager": "ms-python.python:pip",
  "python-envs.workspaceSearchPaths": [],
  "python.useEnvironmentsExtension": false,
  "python.defaultInterpreterPath": "/opt/conda/bin/python",
  "python.condaPath": "/opt/conda/bin/conda",
  "jupyter.jupyterServerType": "local",
  "jupyter.kernels.excludePythonEnvironments": [],
  "python.terminal.activateEnvironment": true,
  "files.watcherExclude": {
    "**/.git/**": true,
    "**/data/**": true,
    "**/results/**": true,
    "**/scratch/**": true
  },
  "search.exclude": {
    "**/data/**": true,
    "**/results/**": true,
    "**/scratch/**": true
  }
}
EOF

cd /
git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/comb
cd comb
pip install -e .
#git checkout 21b9cae9431f7f83e1973af6f1fc1ecd40fc765d
git checkout for_gcamp_validation

cd /
git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/lamf-analysis
cd lamf-analysis
pip install -e .

# Update Fiji
FIJI_DIR=/Fiji.app
FIJI="$FIJI_DIR/ImageJ-linux64"
# Update all components that the Updater would select by default
"$FIJI" --update update

echo "Fiji/BDV ecosystem updated."

v.value:
**Root Cause**
- The slowdown is from Python Environments (PET) timing out repeatedly, not missing Python itself. The logs show repeated configure timeout loops before kernel list is ready.

**What I changed**
- Disabled the new env-discovery path that is timing out in your workspace:

- python.useEnvironmentsExtension: false
  - python-envs.workspaceSearchPaths: []
- Applied the same fix to future capsule boots in

.

**Next step (required once)**
- Run Developer: Reload Window.
- Reopen a notebook and click Select Kernel; it should populate much faster using the pinned interpreter path.

If it is still slow after reload, I can apply one more hard fallback: auto-disable the ms-python.vscode-python-envs extension during postInstall.

toolCallRounds.response:
**Root Cause**
- The main problem is not VS Code version and not profile corruption in [code/vscode-data](code/vscode-data).
- Python kernel discovery was stalling in the Python Environments helper (PET): repeated 30s configure timeouts in [code/vscode-data/logs/20260226T220137/window1/exthost/ms-python.vscode-python-envs/Python Environments.log](code/vscode-data/logs/20260226T220137/window1/exthost/ms-python.vscode-python-envs/Python%20Environments.log#L822) and [same file](code/vscode-data/logs/20260226T220137/window1/exthost/ms-python.vscode-python-envs/Python%20Environments.log#L827).
- PET was configured to scan workspace scope ([same file](code/vscode-data/logs/20260226T220137/window1/exthost/ms-python.vscode-python-envs/Python%20Environments.log#L822)), and this workspace includes a symlinked scratch path.

**Why this capsule hit it**
- In this capsule, /root/capsule/scratch is a symlink to /scratch, and /scratch is NFS-mounted.
- Metadata IO on NFS here is dramatically slower (measured: about 29.7s in /scratch vs 0.14s in local /root/capsule/code for the same file-churn test).
- That matches PET’s fixed 30s timeout behavior almost exactly, which is why detection looked hung/intermittent.

**About moving vscode-data to scratch**
- Putting user-data-dir under /scratch would likely worsen startup and lock/cache operations (NFS latency), so that earlier attempt failing/sluggish is consistent with this environment.

**Why it works now**
- Your current settings in [.vscode/settings.json](.vscode/settings.json) explicitly constrain discovery and disable the timeout-prone env-extension path, so PET no longer blocks kernel availability.

If you want, I can now replace [environment/postInstall](environment/postInstall) with a minimal version (VS Code + Python/Jupyter + 3-4 essential settings) so it is much cleaner and easier to maintain.

                "interp_zstacktoHCR_0 = Rbf(points_zstack[:, 0], points_zstack[:, 1], points_zstack[:, 2], points_HCR[:,0],",

                "interp_zstacktoHCR_1 = Rbf(points_zstack[:, 0], points_zstack[:, 1], points_zstack[:, 2], points_HCR[:,1],",

                "HCR_centroids_est[:,2] = interp_zstacktoHCR_2(czstack_cell_centroids[:, 0],",

        {

            "source": [
                "matched_cells_df = czstack_cell_centroids_df.copy()",
                "",
                "# Initialize columns",
                "n_cz = len(matched_cells_df)",
                "matched_cells_df['hcr_cell_id'] = -1",
                "matched_cells_df['hcr_x'] = np.nan",
                "matched_cells_df['hcr_y'] = np.nan",
                "matched_cells_df['hcr_z'] = np.nan",
                "matched_cells_df['distance'] = np.nan",

                "# Merge once, outside the loop",
                "HCR_df = pd.merge(",
                "    HCR_cell_centroids_df.reset_index(),",
                "    spot_488_counts,",
                "    left_index=True,",
                "    right_index=True",
                ")",
                "",
                "# Convert to NumPy arrays for speed",
                "HCR_ids = HCR_df.index.to_numpy()                          # (N_hcr,)",
                "HCR_coords = HCR_df[['hcr_z', 'hcr_y', 'hcr_x']].to_numpy()  # (N_hcr, 3)",
                "# HCR_value = HCR_df['count'].to_numpy()                    # (N_hcr,)",
                "HCR_value = HCR_df['density'].to_numpy()                    # (N_hcr,)",
                "",
                "N_hcr = len(HCR_ids)",
                "",
                "# CZ coordinates that you are matching against",
                "# (assumes HCR_centroids_est[i] is (z, y, x) for CZ cell i)",
                "CZ_coords = np.asarray(HCR_centroids_est)                  # (N_cz, 3)",
                "",
                "# Track which HCR cells are already used",
                "used = np.zeros(N_hcr, dtype=bool)",
                "",
                "# Storage for assignments (HCR *row positions*, not IDs yet)",
                "assigned_hcr_pos = np.full(n_cz, -1, dtype=int)",
                "assigned_dist = np.full(n_cz, np.nan, dtype=float)",
                "",
                "k = 5  # number of nearest neighbors to consider",
                "",
                "for i, czsid in tqdm(",
                "    enumerate(matched_cells_df.index.values),",
                "    total=n_cz",
                "):",
                "    # Remaining HCR cells (not yet used)",
                "    remaining = np.flatnonzero(~used)",
                "    if remaining.size == 0:",
                "        # No more HCR cells to assign",
                "        break",
                "",
                "    # HCR coords for remaining cells",
                "    rem_coords = HCR_coords[remaining]  # (N_rem, 3)",
                "",
                "    # Distances to current CZ cell",
                "    diff = rem_coords - CZ_coords[i]    # (N_rem, 3)",
                "    dist = np.linalg.norm(diff, axis=1) # (N_rem,)",
                "",
                "    # Take up to k nearest neighbors",
                "    k_eff = min(k, dist.size)",
                "    # argpartition is faster than full argsort",
                "    nearest_local = np.argpartition(dist, k_eff - 1)[:k_eff]",
                "    nearest_rem_idx = remaining[nearest_local]  # positions in full HCR arrays",
                "",
                "    # Among these k neighbors, pick one with max count",
                "    cand_value = HCR_value[nearest_rem_idx]",
                "    best_local = np.argmax(cand_value)",
                "    best_hcr_pos = nearest_rem_idx[best_local]",
                "    best_dist = dist[nearest_local[best_local]]",
                "",
                "    # Record assignment",
                "    assigned_hcr_pos[i] = best_hcr_pos",
                "    assigned_dist[i] = best_dist",
                "",
                "    # Mark this HCR cell as used",
                "    used[best_hcr_pos] = True",
                "",
                "# ------------------------------------------------------------------",
                "# Fill the DataFrame with the results",
                "# ------------------------------------------------------------------",
                "",
                "valid = assigned_hcr_pos >= 0  # in case we ran out of HCR cells",
                "",
                "matched_cells_df = matched_cells_df.loc[valid].copy()",
                "",
                "matched_cells_df['hcr_cell_id'] = HCR_ids[assigned_hcr_pos[valid]]",
                "",
                "# Use coordinates from HCR_coords (z, y, x) and scale",
                "matched_cells_df['hcr_z'] = HCR_coords[assigned_hcr_pos[valid], 0] * scale_z",
                "matched_cells_df['hcr_y'] = HCR_coords[assigned_hcr_pos[valid], 1] * scale_y",
                "matched_cells_df['hcr_x'] = HCR_coords[assigned_hcr_pos[valid], 2] * scale_x",
                "",
                "matched_cells_df['distance'] = assigned_dist[valid]",
                "",
                "# # If you like them sorted by distance (like your original final line):",
                "matched_cells_df = matched_cells_df.sort_values(",
                "    by='distance'",
                ").reset_index()",
                ""
            ]

                "# landmarks_ext[0] = ['Pt-'+str(int(i)) for i in range(len(data))]",

                "# landmarks_ext.loc[matched_cells_df['distance']<5,1] = True",
                "landmarks_ext[2] = matched_cells_df['czstack_x'].values",
                "landmarks_ext[3] = matched_cells_df['czstack_y'].values",
                "landmarks_ext[4] = matched_cells_df['czstack_z'].values",
                "landmarks_ext[5] = matched_cells_df['hcr_x'].values",

        },

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -eu
+
+echo "==== Minimal VS Code + Python/Jupyter bootstrap ===="
+
+TMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
+trap 'rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"' EXIT
+cd "$TMP_DIR"
+
+fetch() {
+  URL="$1"
+  OUT="$2"
+  if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
+  elif command -v wget >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    wget -q "$URL" -O "$OUT"
+  else
+    apt-get update
+    apt-get install -y curl
+    curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
+  fi
+}
+
+echo "Installing VS Code..."
+fetch "https://code.visualstudio.com/sha/download?build=stable&os=linux-deb-x64" "vscode.deb"
+dpkg -i vscode.deb || (apt-get update && apt-get install -y -f && dpkg -i vscode.deb)
+
+mkdir -p /root/capsule/code/vscode-data
+
+echo "Installing Python core packages..."
+python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel
+python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
+  jupyter \
+  ipykernel \
+  numpy==1.26.4 \
+  pandas \
+  scipy \
+  matplotlib \
+  tifffile \
+  zarr \
+  opencv-python-headless==4.11.0.86
+
+echo "Registering Python kernel..."
+python3 -m ipykernel install --sys-prefix --name python3 --display-name "Python 3"
+
+if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  echo "Installing VS Code extensions..."
+  code --install-extension ms-python.python --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
+  code --install-extension ms-toolsai.jupyter --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
+fi
+
+echo "alias code='code --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox'" >> /etc/bash.bashrc
+
+echo "Writing workspace settings for fast kernel detection..."
+mkdir -p /root/capsule/.vscode
+cat > /root/capsule/.vscode/settings.json << 'EOF'
+{
+  "python.useEnvironmentsExtension": false,
+  "python.defaultInterpreterPath": "/opt/conda/bin/python",
+  "python.condaPath": "/opt/conda/bin/conda",
+  "jupyter.jupyterServerType": "local",
+  "python.terminal.activateEnvironment": true,
+  "files.watcherExclude": {
+    "**/.git/**": true,
+    "**/data/**": true,
+    "**/results/**": true,
+    "**/scratch/**": true
+  },
+  "search.exclude": {
+    "**/data/**": true,
+    "**/results/**": true,
+    "**/scratch/**": true
+  }
+}
+EOF
+
+apt-get clean || true
+rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* /var/cache/apt/archives/*
+
+echo "Bootstrap complete."
*** End Patch"}

edits.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu

echo "==== Minimal VS Code + Python/Jupyter bootstrap ===="

TMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"' EXIT
cd "$TMP_DIR"

fetch() {
  URL="$1"
  OUT="$2"
  if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
  elif command -v wget >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    wget -q "$URL" -O "$OUT"
  else
    apt-get update
    apt-get install -y curl
    curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
  fi
}

echo "Installing VS Code..."
fetch "https://code.visualstudio.com/sha/download?build=stable&os=linux-deb-x64" "vscode.deb"
dpkg -i vscode.deb || (apt-get update && apt-get install -y -f && dpkg -i vscode.deb)

mkdir -p /root/capsule/code/vscode-data

echo "Installing Python core packages..."
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel
python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
  jupyter \
  ipykernel \
  numpy==1.26.4 \
  pandas \
  scipy \
  matplotlib \
  tifffile \
  zarr \
  opencv-python-headless==4.11.0.86

echo "Registering Python kernel..."
python3 -m ipykernel install --sys-prefix --name python3 --display-name "Python 3"

if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Installing VS Code extensions..."
  code --install-extension ms-python.python --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
  code --install-extension ms-toolsai.jupyter --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
fi

echo "alias code='code --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox'" >> /etc/bash.bashrc

echo "Writing workspace settings for fast kernel detection..."
mkdir -p /root/capsule/.vscode
cat > /root/capsule/.vscode/settings.json << 'EOF'
{
  "python.useEnvironmentsExtension": false,
  "python.defaultInterpreterPath": "/opt/conda/bin/python",
  "python.condaPath": "/opt/conda/bin/conda",
  "jupyter.jupyterServerType": "local",
  "python.terminal.activateEnvironment": true,
  "files.watcherExclude": {
    "**/.git/**": true,
    "**/data/**": true,
    "**/results/**": true,
    "**/scratch/**": true
  },
  "search.exclude": {
    "**/data/**": true,
    "**/results/**": true,
    "**/scratch/**": true
  }
}
EOF

apt-get clean || true
rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* /var/cache/apt/archives/*

echo "Bootstrap complete."

{"filePath":"/root/capsule/environment/postInstall.full","content":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu

echo "==== Full VS Code + Python/Jupyter bootstrap (backup profile) ===="

TMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"' EXIT
cd "$TMP_DIR"

fetch() {
  URL="$1"
  OUT="$2"
  if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
  elif command -v wget >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    wget -q "$URL" -O "$OUT"
  else
    apt-get update
    apt-get install -y curl
    curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
  fi
}

echo "Installing VS Code..."
fetch "https://code.visualstudio.com/sha/download?build=stable&os=linux-deb-x64" "vscode.deb"
dpkg -i vscode.deb || (apt-get update && apt-get install -y -f && dpkg -i vscode.deb)

if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1 && dpkg -s code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "VS Code installed successfully"
else
  echo "Error: VS Code installation failed"
  exit 1
fi

mkdir -p /root/capsule/code/vscode-data

echo "Installing Python core..."
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel
python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir jupyter ipykernel

echo "Installing VS Code Python/Jupyter extensions..."
code --install-extension ms-python.python --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
code --install-extension ms-toolsai.jupyter --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox

echo "alias code='code --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox'" >> /etc/bash.bashrc

apt-get update
apt-get install -y numlockx gthumb || true

echo "Installing full Python package set..."
python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
  pandas \
  seaborn \
  matplotlib \
  scipy \
  tifffile \
  zarr \
  "numpy==1.26.4" \
  "opencv-python-headless==4.11.0.86" \
  codeocean==0.14.0 \
  aind_session \
  pytest \
  pillow \
  pyyaml \
  aind-ophys-utils \
  pymongo \
  scikit-learn \
  suite2p \
  pyqtgraph \
  pyqt5 \
  'napari[all]' \
  'pydantic<2.11' \
  plotly \
  anywidget \
  ipympl

echo "Registering Python kernel..."
python3 -m ipykernel install --sys-prefix --name python3 --display-name "Python 3"
python3 -m jupyter kernelspec list

echo "Writing workspace settings..."
mkdir -p /root/capsule/.vscode
cat > /root/capsule/.vscode/settings.json << 'EOF'
{
  "python.useEnvironmentsExtension": false,
  "python.defaultInterpreterPath": "/opt/conda/bin/python",
  "python.condaPath": "/opt/conda/bin/conda",
  "jupyter.jupyterServerType": "local",
  "python.terminal.activateEnvironment": true,
  "files.watcherExclude": {
    "**/.git/**": true,
    "**/data/**": true,
    "**/results/**": true,
    "**/scratch/**": true
  },
  "search.exclude": {
    "**/data/**": true,
    "**/results/**": true,
    "**/scratch/**": true
  }
}
EOF

echo "Installing optional repos..."
cd /
git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/comb || true
cd /comb && pip install -e . || true
cd /comb && git checkout for_gcamp_validation || true

git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/lamf-analysis || true
cd /lamf-analysis && pip install -e . || true

echo "Updating Fiji (optional)..."
FIJI_DIR=/Fiji.app
FIJI="$FIJI_DIR/ImageJ-linux64"
if [ -x "$FIJI" ]; then
  "$FIJI" --update update || true
fi

apt-get clean || true
rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* /var/cache/apt/archives/*

echo "Full bootstrap complete."
"}
